blocks.normalized_text: ama naming makapangyarihan lumalapit kami sa lyo na may pagpapakumbaba batid namin na ang karunungan ng tao ay may hangganan ngunit ang lyong katotohanan at katarungan ay nananatili magpakailanman sa pagtupad namin ng aming tungkulin bilang mga senator-judges hiniihiling namin ang lyong gabay sa lahat ng aming gagawin pagkalooban mo nawa kami ng tapat na puso at malinaw na pag-iisip sa buong panahon ng paglilitis na ito panginoon nakikiusap po kaming akayin mo kami sa landas na makatarungan na may patas na pagtingin sa karapatan ng bawat isa dalhin ninyo kami sa liwanag sapagkat nakapiring ang aming mga mata hindi man po namin hiningi ngunit ipinagkatiwala sa aming konstitusyon ang timbangan ng katarungan kumiling nawa ito sa karapat-dapat na pagkalooban nito
blocks.raw_text: Ama naming makapangyarihan, lumalapit kami sa lyo na may pagpapakumbaba. Batid namin na ang karunungan ng tao ay may hangganan, ngunit ang lyong katotohanan at katarungan ay nananatili magpakailanman Sa pagtupad namin ng aming tungkulin bilang mga Senator-Judges, hiniihiling namin ang lyong gabay sa lahat ng aming gagawin. Pagkalooban Mo nawa kami ng tapat na puso at malinaw na pag-iisip sa buong panahon ng paglilitis na ito. Panginoon, nakikiusap po kaming akayin Mo kami sa landas na makatarungan na may patas na pagtingin sa karapatan ng bawat isa. Dalhin Ninyo kami sa liwanag, sapagkat nakapiring ang aming mga mata. Hindi man po namin hiningi ngunit ipinagkatiwala sa aming Konstitusyon ang timbangan ng katarungan. Kumiling nawa ito sa karapat-dapat na pagkalooban nito.
blocks.source_text:
Ama naming makapangyarihan, lumalapit kami sa lyo na may pagpapakumbaba. 
Batid namin na ang karunungan ng tao ay may hangganan, ngunit ang lyong 
katotohanan at katarungan ay nananatili magpakailanman
Sa pagtupad namin ng aming tungkulin bilang mga Senator-Judges, hiniihiling 
namin ang lyong gabay sa lahat ng aming gagawin. Pagkalooban Mo nawa kami 
ng tapat na puso at malinaw na pag-iisip sa buong panahon ng paglilitis na ito. 
Panginoon, nakikiusap po kaming akayin Mo kami sa landas na makatarungan na 
may patas na pagtingin sa karapatan ng bawat isa. Dalhin Ninyo kami sa liwanag, 
sapagkat nakapiring ang aming mga mata. Hindi man po namin hiningi ngunit 
ipinagkatiwala sa aming Konstitusyon ang timbangan ng katarungan. Kumiling 
nawa ito sa karapat-dapat na pagkalooban nito.

blocks.normalized_text: nawa ang inyong salita ang maging gabay sa aming paglilingkod at pagdala sa amin sa katotohanan kagaya po ng winika sa leviticus 19 15 do not pervert justice do not show partiality but judge your neighbor fairly lagi po naming tatandaan ang tagubilin ninyong ito
blocks.raw_text: Nawa ang Inyong Salita ang maging gabay sa aming paglilingkod at pagdala sa amin sa katotohanan. Kagaya po ng winika sa Leviticus 19:15: “Do not pervert justice, do not show partiality, but judge your neighbor fairly.” Lagi po naming tatandaan ang tagubilin Ninyong ito.
blocks.source_text:
Nawa ang Inyong Salita ang maging gabay sa aming paglilingkod at pagdala 
sa amin sa katotohanan. Kagaya po ng winika sa Leviticus 19:15: “Do not pervert 
justice, do not show partiality, but judge your neighbor fairly.” Lagi po naming tatandaan 
ang tagubilin Ninyong ito.

blocks.normalized_text: ama anuman ang maging bunga ng paglilitis na ito hindi nawa ito makita bilang tagumpay ng isa at pagkatalo ng iba kundi para sa ikabubuti ng sambayanang pilipino lahat po ng aming gagawin ay para sa ikalulugod mo at hindi para sa kapakinabangan ng sinumang tao mga partido o kanilang mga pansariling interes kundi ito y para sa pagpapatibay ng aming mga institusyon ng pamahalaan at sa pagpapanatili ng tiwala ng mamamayan sa demokrasya ng aming bayan
blocks.raw_text: Ama, anuman ang maging bunga ng paglilitis na ito, hindi nawa ito makita bilang tagumpay ng isa at pagkatalo ng iba, kundi para sa ikabubuti ng sambayanang Pilipino. Lahat po ng aming gagawin ay para sa ikalulugod Mo at hindi para sa kapakinabangan ng sinumang tao, mga partido, o kanilang mga pansariling interes, kundi ito’y para sa pagpapatibay ng aming mga institusyon, ng pamahalaan, at sa pagpapanatili ng tiwala ng mamamayan sa demokrasya ng aming bayan.
blocks.source_text:
Ama, anuman ang maging bunga ng paglilitis na ito, hindi nawa ito makita 
bilang tagumpay ng isa at pagkatalo ng iba, kundi para sa ikabubuti ng 
sambayanang Pilipino. Lahat po ng aming gagawin ay para sa ikalulugod Mo at 
hindi para sa kapakinabangan ng sinumang tao, mga partido, o kanilang mga 
pansariling interes, kundi ito’y para sa pagpapatibay ng aming mga institusyon, ng 
pamahalaan, at sa pagpapanatili ng tiwala ng mamamayan sa demokrasya ng aming 
bayan.

blocks.normalized_text: mark vinluan regarding his earlier statement that vice president duterte s response to questions about the alleged operation romanov was unconvential but justified considering the threat on her and her family he confirmed whether it was the same operation romanov mentioned by vlogger princess maui during the vice president s online press conference atty vinluan answered in the affirmative
blocks.raw_text: Mark Vinluan, regarding his earlier statement that Vice President Duterte’s response to questions about the alleged “Operation Romanov” was unconvential but justified, considering the threat on her and her family. He confirmed whether it was the same “Operation Romanov” mentioned by vlogger Princess Maui during the Vice President’s online press conference. Atty. Vinluan answered in the affirmative.
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Tulfo (R) sought clarification from the counsel for the defendant, Atty. Mark 
Vinluan, regarding his earlier statement that Vice President Duterte’s response to questions about 
the alleged “Operation Romanov” was unconvential but justified, considering the threat on her and 
her family. He confirmed whether it was the same “Operation Romanov” mentioned by vlogger 
Princess Maui during the Vice President’s online press conference. Atty. Vinluan answered in the 
affirmative.

blocks.normalized_text: senator-judge tulfo r noted that he first heard the term operation romanov during the maisug leaders forum on january 28 2024 in davao city where it was mentioned by mayor sebastian duterte and asked whether it was related to the references made by princess maui and atty vinluan
blocks.raw_text: Senator-Judge Tulfo (R) noted that he first heard the term “Operation Romanov” during the Maisug Leaders’ Forum on January 28, 2024 in Davao City, where it was mentioned by Mayor Sebastian Duterte, and asked whether it was related to the references made by Princess Maui and Atty. Vinluan.
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Tulfo (R) noted that he first heard the term “Operation Romanov” during 
the Maisug Leaders’ Forum on January 28, 2024 in Davao City, where it was mentioned by 
Mayor Sebastian Duterte, and asked whether it was related to the references made by 
Princess Maui and Atty. Vinluan.

blocks.normalized_text: in reply attorney vinluan said he could not comment since he had no personal knowledge of mayor duterte s statement when asked what he meant when he said that the vice president s response as unconventional attorney vinluan explained that the term was merely a description of the statements she made during the press conference and that the word should be understood in its ordinary meaning
blocks.raw_text: In reply. Attorney Vinluan said he could not comment since he had no personal knowledge of Mayor Duterte’s statement. When asked what he meant when he said that the Vice President’s response as unconventional. Attorney Vinluan explained that the term was merely a description of the statements she made during the press conference and that the word should be understood in its ordinary meaning.
blocks.source_text:
In reply. Attorney Vinluan said he could not comment since he had no personal knowledge of 
Mayor Duterte’s statement. When asked what he meant when he said that the Vice President’s 
response as unconventional. Attorney Vinluan explained that the term was merely a description 
of the statements she made during the press conference and that the word should be understood 
in its ordinary meaning.

blocks.normalized_text: before the examination of the prosecution s second witness prosecutor defensor informed the court that they received a letter dated july 10 2026 from director melvin a matibag of the national bureau of investigation nbi requesting that his testimony be scheduled at the earliest opportunity due to his attendance in a regional summit on scam center compounds and transnational organized crime organized by the federal bureau of investigation fbi and the royal thai police he requested that director matibag testify the following day immediately after the testimony of the prosecution s second witness atty jeremy a lotoc
blocks.raw_text: Before the examination of the prosecution’s second witness. Prosecutor Defensor informed the Court that they received a letter dated July 10, 2026, from Director Melvin A. Matibag of the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI), requesting that his testimony be scheduled at the earliest opportunity due to his attendance in a regional summit on scam center compounds and transnational organized crime organized by the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) and the Royal Thai Police. He requested that Director Matibag testify the following day immediately after the testimony of the prosecution’s second witness, Atty. Jeremy A. Lotoc.
blocks.source_text:
Before the examination of the prosecution’s second witness. Prosecutor Defensor informed the 
Court that they received a letter dated July 10, 2026, from Director Melvin A. Matibag of the 
National Bureau of Investigation (NBI), requesting that his testimony be scheduled at the earliest 
opportunity due to his attendance in a regional summit on scam center compounds and transnational 
organized crime organized by the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) and the Royal Thai Police. 
He requested that Director Matibag testify the following day immediately after the testimony of the 
prosecution’s second witness, Atty. Jeremy A. Lotoc.

blocks.normalized_text: the presiding officer sought clarification noting that atty zuleika lopez chief of staff of the office of the vice president ovp had previously been scheduled to testify the following day prosecutor defensor confirmed the existing schedule and explained that after director matibag s testimony the proceedings would continue with the previously announced order of witnesses including attorney lopez and capt belinda bello head of the legislative security bureau of the house of representatives hor
blocks.raw_text: The Presiding Officer sought clarification, noting that Atty. Zuleika Lopez, Chief of Staff of the Office of the Vice President (OVP) had previously been scheduled to testify the following day. Prosecutor Defensor confirmed the existing schedule and explained that, after Director Matibag’s testimony, the proceedings would continue with the previously announced order of witnesses, including Attorney Lopez and Capt. Belinda Bello, head of the Legislative Security Bureau of the House of Representatives (HOR).
blocks.source_text:
The Presiding Officer sought clarification, noting that Atty. Zuleika Lopez, Chief of Staff of the 
Office of the Vice President (OVP) had previously been scheduled to testify the following day. 
Prosecutor Defensor confirmed the existing schedule and explained that, after Director Matibag’s 
testimony, the proceedings would continue with the previously announced order of witnesses, 
including Attorney Lopez and Capt. Belinda Bello, head of the Legislative Security Bureau of the 
House of Representatives (HOR).

blocks.normalized_text: senator-judge cayetano a stated that he would not object to the request if there were no objections from the members but expressed concern over accommodating the request cautioning that allowing a bureau head to dictate the court s schedule could set a bad precedent and affect the orderly presentation of evidence he emphasized that the senator-judges had likewise set aside official responsibilities to attend the impeachment trial
blocks.raw_text: Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) stated that he would not object to the request if there were no objections from the Members but expressed concern over accommodating the request, cautioning that allowing a bureau head to dictate the Court’s schedule could set a bad precedent and affect the orderly presentation of evidence. He emphasized that the senator-judges had likewise set aside official responsibilities to attend the impeachment trial.
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) stated that he would not object to the request if there were no 
objections from the Members but expressed concern over accommodating the request, cautioning 
that allowing a bureau head to dictate the Court’s schedule could set a bad precedent and affect 
the orderly presentation of evidence. He emphasized that the senator-judges had likewise set aside 
official responsibilities to attend the impeachment trial.

blocks.normalized_text: in response prosecutor defensor explained that it was only requesting to advance director matibag s testimony by one day without altering the sequence of the remaining witnesses while stating that the prosecution would abide by the court s ruling
blocks.raw_text: In response. Prosecutor Defensor explained that it was only requesting to advance Director Matibag’s testimony by one day without altering the sequence of the remaining witnesses, while stating that the prosecution would abide by the Court’s ruling.
blocks.source_text:
In response. Prosecutor Defensor explained that it was only requesting to advance Director 
Matibag’s testimony by one day without altering the sequence of the remaining witnesses, while 
stating that the prosecution would abide by the Court’s ruling.

blocks.normalized_text: the presiding officer recalled the prosecution s previous statements that director matibag would not testify during the current week and that only attorney lotoc attorney lopez and captain bello will be presented as this week s witnesses he pointed out that director matibag had not been included in the witness list and that no subpoena had been issued requiring his appearance during the week correcting the statement that the testimony is merely being advanced by one day
blocks.raw_text: The Presiding Officer recalled the prosecution’s previous statements that Director Matibag would not testify during the current week and that only Attorney Lotoc, Attorney Lopez, and Captain Bello will be presented as this week’s witnesses. He pointed out that Director Matibag had not been included in the witness list and that no subpoena had been issued requiring his appearance during the week, correcting the statement that the testimony is merely being advanced by one day.
blocks.source_text:
The Presiding Officer recalled the prosecution’s previous statements that Director Matibag 
would not testify during the current week and that only Attorney Lotoc, Attorney Lopez, and 
Captain Bello will be presented as this week’s witnesses. He pointed out that Director Matibag had 
not been included in the witness list and that no subpoena had been issued requiring his appearance 
during the week, correcting the statement that the testimony is merely being advanced by one day.

blocks.normalized_text: prosecutor defensor clarified that while the prosecution had earlier identified its witnesses for the week it also informed the court that it would later specify the date of director matibag s testimony after requesting the issuance of a subpoena he said the prosecution had intended to present director matibag on wednesday after the testimony of captain bello the presiding officer acknowledged the explanation but noted that in light of the one-witness-per-day rule
blocks.raw_text: Prosecutor Defensor clarified that, while the prosecution had earlier identified its witnesses for the week, it also informed the Court that it would later specify the date of Director Matibag’s testimony after requesting the issuance of a subpoena. He said the prosecution had intended to present Director Matibag on Wednesday, after the testimony of Captain Bello. The Presiding Officer acknowledged the explanation but noted that, in light of the one-witness-per-day rule.
blocks.source_text:
Prosecutor Defensor clarified that, while the prosecution had earlier identified its witnesses for 
the week, it also informed the Court that it would later specify the date of Director Matibag’s 
testimony after requesting the issuance of a subpoena. He said the prosecution had intended to 
present Director Matibag on Wednesday, after the testimony of Captain Bello. The Presiding 
Officer acknowledged the explanation but noted that, in light of the one-witness-per-day rule.

no subpoena had been issued to Director Matibag for the week. Upon the Presiding Officer’s

blocks.normalized_text: senator-judge cayetano a clarified that his earlier remarks were not directed at the prosecution but recalled that the change in schedule affects the preparation of the senator-judges after seeking clarification on the applicable notice rules from the presiding officer he reiterated the importance of adhering to the court s procedures to ensure the orderly and efficient conduct of the impeachment trial
blocks.raw_text: Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) clarified that his earlier remarks were not directed at the prosecution but recalled that the change in schedule affects the preparation of the senator-judges. After seeking clarification on the applicable notice rules from the Presiding Officer, he reiterated the importance of adhering to the Court’s procedures to ensure the orderly and efficient conduct of the impeachment trial.
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) clarified that his earlier remarks were not directed at the 
prosecution but recalled that the change in schedule affects the preparation of the senator-judges. 
After seeking clarification on the applicable notice rules from the Presiding Officer, he reiterated 
the importance of adhering to the Court’s procedures to ensure the orderly and efficient conduct 
of the impeachment trial.

blocks.normalized_text: attorney vinluan opposed the prosecution s request to advance the testimony of director matibag arguing that it would violate the court s previous directive on the order of witnesses the five-day notice requirement and the principles of fair play and procedural due process he maintained that the defense had prepared its case based on the previously announced witness schedule and that a last-minute change would prejudice its ability to conduct an effective cross-examination presiding officer escudero clarified that the five-day notice period is for witnesses not mentioned in the pre-trial order
blocks.raw_text: Attorney Vinluan opposed the prosecution’s request to advance the testimony of Director Matibag, arguing that it would violate the Court’s previous directive on the order of witnesses, the five-day notice requirement, and the principles of fair play and procedural due process. He maintained that the defense had prepared its case based on the previously announced witness schedule and that a last-minute change would prejudice its ability to conduct an effective cross- examination. Presiding Officer Escudero clarified that the five-day notice period is for witnesses not mentioned in the pre-trial order.
blocks.source_text:
Attorney Vinluan opposed the prosecution’s request to advance the testimony of Director 
Matibag, arguing that it would violate the Court’s previous directive on the order of witnesses, the 
five-day notice requirement, and the principles of fair play and procedural due process. He 
maintained that the defense had prepared its case based on the previously announced witness 
schedule and that a last-minute change would prejudice its ability to conduct an effective cross- 
examination. Presiding Officer Escudero clarified that the five-day notice period is for witnesses 
not mentioned in the pre-trial order.

blocks.normalized_text: attorney vinluan also requested that the prosecution provide the defense with a copy of director matibag s invitation to the international conference and disclose whether a corresponding travel authority had been issued he explained that the defense had received information that a similar fbi regional summit had already been held in bangkok the previous month and wished to verify whether director matibag s attendance justified the requested modification of the witness schedule
blocks.raw_text: Attorney Vinluan also requested that the prosecution provide the defense with a copy of Director Matibag’s invitation to the international conference and disclose whether a corresponding travel authority had been issued. He explained that the defense had received information that a similar FBI regional summit had already been held in Bangkok the previous month and wished to verify whether Director Matibag’s attendance justified the requested modification of the witness schedule.
blocks.source_text:
Attorney Vinluan also requested that the prosecution provide the defense with a copy of 
Director Matibag’s invitation to the international conference and disclose whether a corresponding 
travel authority had been issued. He explained that the defense had received information that a 
similar FBI regional summit had already been held in Bangkok the previous month and wished to 
verify whether Director Matibag’s attendance justified the requested modification of the witness 
schedule.

He noted that many senator-judges devoted the weekend to studying the expected testimonies and preparing their questions based on the previously announced witness schedule.
blocks.source_text:
Senate President Gatchalian expressed support for the manifestation of Senator-Judge Cayetano 
(A), emphasizing that the senator-judges, like the prosecution and defense, prepare in advance for 
the scheduled presentation of witnesses. He noted that many senator-judges devoted the weekend 
to studying the expected testimonies and preparing their questions based on the previously 
announced witness schedule.

blocks.normalized_text: senate president gatchalian reiterated that the senator-judges devoted considerable time to reviewing the voluminous documentary evidence and preparing questions for the scheduled witnesses he cautioned that the last-minute inclusion of additional witnesses would deprive the senator-judges of sufficient time to study the evidence and prepare their inquiries thereby affecting the orderly and fair conduct of the proceedings
blocks.raw_text: Senate President Gatchalian reiterated that the senator-judges devoted considerable time to reviewing the voluminous documentary evidence and preparing questions for the scheduled witnesses. He cautioned that the last-minute inclusion of additional witnesses would deprive the senator-judges of sufficient time to study the evidence and prepare their inquiries, thereby affecting the orderly and fair conduct of the proceedings.
blocks.source_text:
Senate President Gatchalian reiterated that the senator-judges devoted considerable time to 
reviewing the voluminous documentary evidence and preparing questions for the scheduled 
witnesses. He cautioned that the last-minute inclusion of additional witnesses would deprive the 
senator-judges of sufficient time to study the evidence and prepare their inquiries, thereby affecting 
the orderly and fair conduct of the proceedings.

blocks.source_text:
Reading the transcript verbatim. Presiding Officer Escudero confirmed the prosecution’s earlier 
manifestation that while Director Melvin Matibag had not been scheduled to testify during the week, 
it would determine by today whether to present him on Wednesday, depending on the progress 
of the trial.

blocks.source_text: ORDER OF THE PRESIDING OFFICER

blocks.normalized_text: deferring action on the prosecution s request the presiding officer directed the prosecution to submit director matibag s letter furnishing copies to the respondent s counsel upon the defense s manifestation he further ordered the prosecution to submit any available travel authority and supporting documents including the official invitation from the foreign organizers if already issued he clarified that the documents would be considered solely for the court s evaluation of the request and directed that the defense be furnished copies and given the opportunity to comment before the court rules on the matter
blocks.raw_text: Deferring action on the prosecution’s request, the Presiding Officer directed the prosecution to submit Director Matibag’s letter, furnishing copies to the respondent’s counsel. Upon the defense’s manifestation, he further ordered the prosecution to submit any available travel authority and supporting documents, including the official invitation from the foreign organizers, if already issued. He clarified that the documents would be considered solely for the Court’s evaluation of the request and directed that the defense be furnished copies and given the opportunity to comment before the Court rules on the matter.
blocks.source_text:
Deferring action on the prosecution’s request, the Presiding Officer directed the prosecution 
to submit Director Matibag’s letter, furnishing copies to the respondent’s counsel. Upon the 
defense’s manifestation, he further ordered the prosecution to submit any available travel authority 
and supporting documents, including the official invitation from the foreign organizers, if already 
issued. He clarified that the documents would be considered solely for the Court’s evaluation of 
the request and directed that the defense be furnished copies and given the opportunity to comment 
before the Court rules on the matter.

blocks.section_title: PRESENTATION OF THE PROSECUTION’S SECOND WITNESS
blocks.source_text:
The Presiding Officer directed the prosecution to proceed with the presentation of its second 
witness and recognized Atty. Virgil Ligutan, counsel for the prosecution, to conduct the examination. 
Attorney Ligutan called on Atty. Jeremy C. Lotoc of the NBI to the witness stand, after which 
the Clerk of Court administered his oath.

blocks.source_text: OFFER OF COUNSEL FOR THE PROSECUTION

blocks.normalized_text: attorney ligutan offered the testimony of attorney lotoc to establish his position as chief of the nbi cybercrime division in 2024 his participation in the bureau s investigation into the respondent s alleged threats during the november 23 2024 online media briefing the nbi s findings that the respondent s statements constituted serious and credible threats which led to the filing of complaints for three counts of grave threats and one count of inciting to sedition and the authenticity of the nbi affidavit of investigation its annexes related video recordings and other matters material to the impeaehment proceedings
blocks.raw_text: Attorney Ligutan offered the testimony of Attorney Lotoc to establish his position as Chief of the NBI Cybercrime Division in 2024; his participation in the bureau’s investigation into the respondent’s alleged threats during the November 23, 2024 online media briefing; the NBI’s findings that the respondent’s statements constituted serious and credible threats, which led to the filing of complaints for three counts of grave threats and one count of inciting to sedition; and the authenticity of the NBI affidavit of investigation, its annexes, related video recordings, and other matters material to the impeaehment proceedings.
blocks.section_title: OFFER OF COUNSEL FOR THE PROSECUTION
blocks.source_text:
Attorney Ligutan offered the testimony of Attorney Lotoc to establish his position as Chief of 
the NBI Cybercrime Division in 2024; his participation in the bureau’s investigation into the 
respondent’s alleged threats during the November 23, 2024 online media briefing; the NBI’s 
findings that the respondent’s statements constituted serious and credible threats, which led to the 
filing of complaints for three counts of grave threats and one count of inciting to sedition; and the 
authenticity of the NBI affidavit of investigation, its annexes, related video recordings, and other 
matters material to the impeaehment proceedings.

blocks.normalized_text: well under the implementing rules and regulations of republic act 10175 or the reorganization and modernization act of nbi hash value refers to mathematical algorithm applied to the information like the file the physical disk or the logical disk in this case the digital evidence thereby creating a digital fingerprint or dna for that information or digital evidence your honor
blocks.raw_text: Well, under the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act 10175, or the Reorganization and Modernization Act of NBI, hash value refers to mathematical algorithm applied to the information, like the file, the physical disk, or the logical disk—in this case, the digital evidence—thereby creating a digital fingerprint or DNA for that information or digital evidence. Your Honor.

to the information, like the file, the physical disk, or the logical disk—in this case, the digital 
evidence—thereby creating a digital fingerprint or DNA for that information or digital evidence. 
Your Honor.

blocks.raw_text: The Philippine National Police issued a statement relative to the press conference made by the Vice President, “The Philippine National Police treats any threat to the safety and security of the President, and all public officials, with the utmost seriousness. In light of the Vice President Sara Duterte’s recent statement, which directly referenced an assassin contracted to target the President, the PNP recognizes the grave nature of this matter. The safety of the President is a national concern, and any direct or indirect threat to his life must be addressed with the highest level of urgency.” Hindi ko na binasa lahat, Your Honor.
blocks.source_text:
The Philippine National Police issued a statement relative to the press conference made by the 
Vice President, “The Philippine National Police treats any threat to the safety and security of the 
President, and all public officials, with the utmost seriousness. In light of the Vice President Sara 
Duterte’s recent statement, which directly referenced an assassin contracted to target the President, 
the PNP recognizes the grave nature of this matter. The safety of the President is a national 
concern, and any direct or indirect threat to his life must be addressed with the highest level of 
urgency.” Hindi ko na binasa lahat, Your Honor.

blocks.normalized_text: your honor insofar as the cybercrime division is concerned we focus on the investigation on the alleged grave threats and inciting to sedition ngayon pagdating doon nang whether or not mayroong na-execute na o may inutusan i think ang makakasagot po niyan your honor iyong iyong aming director kasi sa kanya po iyong pool ng information pero at the same time iyong iyong jurisdiction ho na iyan nasa intelligence service po namin kami ho nasa nasa investigation service so insofar as the nbi cybercrime division is concerned at that time naka-focus ho kami sa grave threats and inciting to sedition pero insofar as whether mayroon na bang a-engage ang bise presidente jurisdiction ho iyan ng aming intelligence service but maybe ang makakasagot niyan iyong aming director kasi siya po iyong recipient ng pool of information from different divisions and regions
blocks.raw_text: Your Honor, insofar as the Cybercrime Division is concerned, we focus on the investigation on the alleged Grave Threats and Inciting to Sedition. Ngayon, pagdating doon nang whether or not mayroong na-execute na o may inutusan, I think ang makakasagot po niyan. Your Honor, iyong—iyong aming Director, kasi sa kanya po iyong pool ng information. Pero at the same time, iyong—iyong jurisdiction ho na iyan nasa Intelligence Service po namin. Kami ho nasa, nasa Investigation Service. So, insofar as the NBI Cybercrime Division is concerned, at that time, naka-focus ho kami sa Grave Threats and Inciting to Sedition. Pero, insofar as whether mayroon na bang «a-engage ang Bise Presidente, jurisdiction ho iyan ng aming Intelligence Service. But maybe ang makakasagot niyan, iyong aming Director, kasi siya po iyong recipient ng pool of information from different divisions and regions.
blocks.source_text:
Mr. Lotoc. Your Honor, insofar as the Cybercrime Division is concerned, we focus on the 
investigation on the alleged Grave Threats and Inciting to Sedition. Ngayon, pagdating doon nang 
whether or not mayroong na-execute na o may inutusan, I think ang makakasagot po niyan. Your 
Honor, iyong—iyong aming Director, kasi sa kanya po iyong pool ng information. Pero at the 
same time, iyong—iyong jurisdiction ho na iyan nasa Intelligence Service po namin. Kami ho nasa, 
nasa Investigation Service. So, insofar as the NBI Cybercrime Division is concerned, at that time, 
naka-focus ho kami sa Grave Threats and Inciting to Sedition. Pero, insofar as whether mayroon 
na bang «a-engage ang Bise Presidente, jurisdiction ho iyan ng aming Intelligence Service. But 
maybe ang makakasagot niyan, iyong aming Director, kasi siya po iyong recipient ng pool of 
information from different divisions and regions.

Bureau of Investigation. Kung hindi po ma-identify o ma-locate ang sinasabing “assassin,” does 
that automatically mean that the threat is no longer credible or actionable, or does the NBI still 
consider the statements uttered and the surrounding circumstances and the totality of the evidence 
before deciding to proceed with further investigation?

in which they were spoken, the person to whom they were addressed, the relationship between the 
parties, and the reactions of the recipients of those utterances. Your Honor.

Senator-Judge Aquino raised a point of order, stating that he had earlier asked the defense to 
identify the specific portion of its pleading stating that the Vice President had not approached any 
person to carry out the alleged act. He then inquired whether the matter could instead be taken 
up after the break. Presiding Officer Escudero asked whether the point referred to his earlier 
inquiry, to which Senator-Judge Aquino replied in the affirmative.

The Presiding Officer informed the Members that, after the remaining senator-judges had 
been given the opportunity to ask their questions, the Court would suspend the proceedings for a 
15-minute break and resume at 5:00 p.m. He further announced that, upon resumption, the Chair 
would recognize the senator-judges for their questions in the following order. Senator-Judge Tulfo, 
Senate President Gatchalian, Senator-Judge Cayetano (A), and Senator-Judge Marcos.

blocks.normalized_text: attorney vinluan manifested that the date 06 november 2024 appeared not only in paragraph 14 on page 6 of the affidavit of investigation but also in footnote no 12 which referred to a subpoena to an abs-cbn news personnel on 06 november 2024 he further pointed out that paragraph 14 likewise stated that a subpoena had been issued to abs-cbn news on the same date for the submission of a copy of the video of the october 18 2024 press conference he added that the subpoena itself included an attachment showing a photograph taken during the press briefing in view thereof he argued that the discrepancy could not be dismissed as a mere typographical error
blocks.raw_text: Attorney Vinluan manifested that the date “06 November 2024” appeared not only in paragraph 14 on page 6 of the Affidavit of Investigation but also in Footnote No. 12, which referred to a subpoena to an ABS-CBN News personnel on 06 November 2024. He further pointed out that paragraph 14 likewise stated that a subpoena had been issued to ABS-CBN News on the same date for the submission of a copy of the video of the October 18, 2024 press conference. He added that the subpoena itself included an attachment showing a photograph taken during the press briefing. In view thereof, he argued that the discrepancy could not be dismissed as a mere typographical error.
blocks.source_text:
Attorney Vinluan manifested that the date “06 November 2024” appeared not only in paragraph 
14 on page 6 of the Affidavit of Investigation but also in Footnote No. 12, which referred to a 
subpoena to an ABS-CBN News personnel on 06 November 2024. He further pointed out that 
paragraph 14 likewise stated that a subpoena had been issued to ABS-CBN News on the same 
date for the submission of a copy of the video of the October 18, 2024 press conference. He added 
that the subpoena itself included an attachment showing a photograph taken during the press 
briefing. In view thereof, he argued that the discrepancy could not be dismissed as a mere 
typographical error.

blocks.normalized_text: attorney ligutan objected maintaining that the witness had already testified that the discrepancy was merely a typographical error

blocks.source_text:
Attorney Ligutan objected, maintaining that the witness had already testified that the discrepancy 
was merely a typographical error.

blocks.normalized_text: with respect to the counter-manifestation of counsel for the prosecutors he observed that the witness had already testified that from his perspective the discrepancy was merely a typographical error
blocks.raw_text: With respect to the counter-manifestation of counsel for the prosecutors, he observed that the witness had already testified that, from his perspective, the discrepancy was merely a typographical error.
blocks.source_text:
Presiding Officer Escudero noted the manifestation of counsel for the respondent. With respect

blocks.raw_text: Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) observed that Article IV of the Articles of Impeachment alleged that Vice President Duterte committed culpable violation of the Constitution, high crimes, and betrayal of public trust by contracting for the assassination of the President, the First Lady, and the former Speaker of the HOR, as well as by making grave threats and actively inciting sedition. He noted that, from the prosecution’s standpoint, the alleged threats themselves, whether constituting high crimes, grave threats, or culpable violations of the Constitution were being treated as impeachable offenses.
blocks.source_text:
Senator-Judge Cayetano (A) observed that Article IV of the Articles of Impeachment alleged 
that Vice President Duterte committed culpable violation of the Constitution, high crimes, and 
betrayal of public trust by contracting for the assassination of the President, the First Lady, and the 
former Speaker of the HOR, as well as by making grave threats and actively inciting sedition. He 
noted that, from the prosecution’s standpoint, the alleged threats themselves, whether constituting 
high crimes, grave threats, or culpable violations of the Constitution were being treated as 
impeachable offenses.

blocks.raw_text: He pointed out, however, that the Vice President did not explicitly state that she had contracted an assassin but merely stated that she had spoken to someone. In response to the Presiding Officer’s inquiry. Attorney Vinluan informed the Court that the 
cross-examination was approximately halfway through. He also noted that it was the first 
opportunity for the Respondent, through Counsel, to confront the witnesses against her, explaining 
that she had not participated in the proceedings before the Committee on Justice of the HOR for 
the reasons stated in her petition for certiorari.

Presiding Officer Escudero clarified that the Court was not limiting the Respondent’s right to 
complete the cross-examination but was merely determining whether to continue that evening or 
resume the proceedings the following day. Attorney Vinluan replied that the Respondent was 
amenable to either course but, in deference to the request of Senator-Judge Zubiri, agreed to 
continue the cross-examination the following day.

Presiding Officer Escudero informed the Court that it had received the Respondent’s 
memorandum on the requests for the issuance of subpoenas duces tecum for bank records, 
records of the Anti-Money Laundering Council (AMLC), and the Bureau of Internal Revenue 
(BIR). Upon inquiry, the Prosecution confirmed that it had likewise submitted its memorandum.
